subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2026, EC, Eurostat, ECB, RSF, TI, WHO) + Cypriot official (CyStat, CBC) + press; assesses the Republic of Cyprus, with the unresolved division of the island a geopolitical factor.
A eurozone democracy with a strong post-2013-crisis recovery, dogged by a frozen division (Turkish troops in the north since 1974) and acute water/climate stress.

Standout features: a sharp fiscal/banking recovery and low crime, against the frozen division, golden-passport-era corruption legacy, and severe water scarcity. Source-lean: MODERATE-HIGH Anglophone on the division narrative.

economic.notes: A strong post-crisis fiscal and banking recovery, with a services-concentrated base.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt down from the 2013-crisis peak to ~55% GDP and falling, with a sizeable surplus. (IMF Art IV 2026; EC)

monetary_independence.notes: Pooled at the ECB - no own policy lever. (ECB)

inflation.notes: Often among the EU's lowest, ticking up modestly. (Eurostat HICP)

banking_stability.notes: Rebuilt since the 2013 bail-in (low NPLs, high capital), with Russian de-risking advanced but a reputational legacy. (CBC; MONEYVAL)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Services-concentrated (tourism, shipping, financial/professional, tech relocation) with East-Med gas upside; small and undiversified. (EC)

institutional.notes: EU-standard courts and credible statistics, against corruption and the golden-passport legacy.

rule_of_law.notes: EU-standard courts but persistent corruption and a weakly-prosecuted golden-passport scandal (the scheme scrapped). (EC Rule of Law 2025; TI)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Functional, with reform and digitalisation ongoing. (EC; OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Free but with a falling RSF rank, ownership concentration, and self-censorship. (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: A stable EU democracy with strong liberties, against migration pressure and the island's division.

political_stability.notes: A stable EU democracy with peaceful turnover. (EC)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A lawful EU framework but a frontline asylum entry point (a high per-capita rate) with deterrence-leaning policy. (Eurostat; ECRE)

social_cohesion.notes: Cohesive in the south, but the island's division is the structural cleavage. (ICG)

demographics.notes: Ageing with low fertility, supported by positive net migration. (CyStat; Eurostat)

geopolitical.notes: Deepening Eastern-Med partnerships, against the frozen, unresolved division.

alliance_reliability.notes: An EU member but non-NATO, with deepening US/Israel/Greece (3+1) defence and energy ties. (US State; FDD)

conflict_involvement.notes: A frozen division - Turkish troops have occupied the north since 1974 - with recurring maritime/gas disputes, a real unresolved tail. (ICG; Atlantic Council)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account; Russian-money de-risking ongoing. (EC; CBC)

physical_practical.notes: Good infrastructure and low crime, against severe water scarcity and heat.

climate.notes: An Eastern-Med hotspot - among the fastest-warming, severe drought/water scarcity, and extreme heat approaching critical. (IPCC AR6; EEA)

healthcare.notes: A universal system (GESY) since 2019, with rising public spend. (WHO Europe)

infrastructure.notes: Good roads, telecom, and ports, with a desalination buildout. (EC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Drought/seismic exposure with a moderately deep market. (press)

summary: A eurozone democracy that staged a strong recovery from its 2013 banking crisis - debt down sharply with surpluses, rebuilt banks, low crime, a new universal-health system, and a services economy (tourism, shipping, finance, tech relocation) with East-Med gas upside. The offsets are persistent corruption and a weakly-prosecuted golden-passport legacy, an unresolved frozen division with Turkish troops in the north since 1974, frontline asylum pressure, and acute water scarcity and heat. Membership of the euro pools monetary policy at a credible ECB; the island is an EU but non-NATO state deepening Eastern-Mediterranean partnerships.
